extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true.

rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires seizure_history AND pregnancy AND polypharmacy. There is no other rule for rec_drug_a, and its default is false. So rec_drug_a = true requires exactly that conjunction C = (seizure_history AND pregnancy AND polypharmacy).

rec_drug_b is affected by two rules: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF seizure_history THEN true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF C THEN false). Conflict resolution is per-output by priority, highest wins.

Case 1: C is false. Then rec_a_from_conjunction does not fire, and no other rule sets rec_drug_a, so rec_drug_a = false (default). The conjunction (a AND b) is false regardless of b. Property holds.

Case 2: C is true. Then rec_drug_a = true. But for rec_drug_b, both rec_b_from_trigger (true, priority 50) and guard_suppress_b (false, priority 90) match; the priority-90 guard wins, so rec_drug_b = false. Thus (a AND b) = (true AND false) = false. Property holds.

Since C true is precisely the only way to get rec_drug_a = true, and C true always forces rec_drug_b false via the higher-priority guard, the two outputs can never both be true across all 64 input combinations. The property holds.
